about.en: Nintendo's flagship store in Rockefeller Center featuring exclusive merchandise, playable demos, and life-size character statues across two floors.

text.en: Two floors of merchandise and playable demos
Hosts special midnight launch events
Photo opportunities with character statues on both floors

address.en: 10 Rockefeller Plaza, New York, NY 10020

category.ja: 任天堂オフィシャルストア

about.en: Nintendo's first official store in Japan, located in Shibuya PARCO. Offers exclusive Tokyo-only merchandise and seasonal limited items.
about.ja: 渋谷PARCOにある任天堂初の公式ストア。東京限定グッズや季節限定アイテムを販売。

text.en: Located in Shibuya PARCO 6F
Exclusive Tokyo-only merchandise available
Seasonal limited-edition items rotate frequently
text.ja: 季節限定アイテムが頻繁に入れ替わる

address.en: Shibuya PARCO 6F, 15-1 Udagawacho, Shibuya, Tokyo
address.ja: 東京都渋谷区宇田川町15-1 渋谷PARCO 6F

category.ja: 任天堂オフィシャルストア

about.en: Nintendo's official store in Osaka's Daimaru Shinsaibashi department store, featuring regional exclusives and a wide range of merchandise.

text.en: Located in Daimaru Shinsaibashi 13F
Osaka-exclusive merchandise available
Generally less crowded than the Tokyo location

address.en: Daimaru Shinsaibashi 13F, 1-7-1 Shinsaibashisuji, Chuo-ku, Osaka

category.ja: 任天堂オフィシャルストア

about.en: Nintendo's store in the Takashimaya department store in Kyoto, the company's hometown. Features Kyoto-exclusive items and a warm, traditional atmosphere.

text.en: Located in Kyoto Takashimaya 5F
Kyoto-exclusive merchandise with traditional motifs
Closest official store to Nintendo's headquarters

address.en: Kyoto Takashimaya 5F, 52 Shincho, Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto

name.ja: ポケモンセンターメガトウキョー

about.en: One of the largest Pokémon Center stores in Japan, offering an extensive selection of plush toys, trading cards, apparel, and exclusive items.

text.en: Located in Sunshine City, Ikebukuro
Massive selection of plush toys and trading cards
Features a life-size Pikachu photo spot

address.en: Sunshine City Alpa 2F, 3-1-2 Higashiikebukuro, Toshima-ku, Tokyo

name.ja: ポケモンセンターシブヤ

about.en: A stylish Pokémon Center in Shibuya PARCO with a modern urban design. Features Mewtwo as its store icon and carries exclusive Shibuya-themed goods.

text.en: Located in Shibuya PARCO 5F, one floor below Nintendo Tokyo
Urban-themed exclusive merchandise
Features Mewtwo as the store's mascot icon

address.en: Shibuya PARCO 5F, 15-1 Udagawacho, Shibuya, Tokyo

name.ja: ポケモンセンターオーサカDX

about.en: A large Pokémon Center in Grand Front Osaka featuring a Pokémon Café adjacent to it. Offers exclusive Osaka-region merchandise and events.

text.en: Located in Grand Front Osaka South Building 9F
Adjacent to a dedicated Pokémon Café
Hosts regular trading card game tournaments

address.en: Grand Front Osaka South Building 9F, 4-20 Ofukacho, Kita-ku, Osaka

name.ja: ポケモンストア東京駅店

about.en: A compact Pokémon Store inside Tokyo Station's Character Street, perfect for grabbing Pokémon souvenirs before catching the Shinkansen.

text.en: Located in Tokyo Station First Avenue Character Street
Great for last-minute souvenir shopping
Carries Tokyo Station-exclusive items

address.en: Tokyo Station First Avenue B1, 1-9-1 Marunouchi, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo

name.ja: ポケモンストア成田空港店

about.en: An airport Pokémon Store in Narita Terminal 2, offering travelers a last chance to pick up Pokémon merchandise before departing Japan.

address.en: Narita International Airport Terminal 2, Narita, Chiba

name.ja: スーパー・ニンテンドー・ワールド

about.en: The original Super Nintendo World at Universal Studios Japan. An immersive area that brings the Mushroom Kingdom to life with rides, interactive experiences, and themed dining.

text.en: Power-Up Bands enable interactive coin collecting
Features Mario Kart: Koopa's Challenge ride
Includes Yoshi's Adventure family ride
Donkey Kong Country area expansion opened in 2024

address.en: 2-1-33 Sakurajima, Konohana-ku, Osaka

hours.en: Varies by season — check USJ website

about.en: The first Super Nintendo World outside Japan, located at Universal Studios Hollywood. Features Mario Kart: Bowser's Challenge and immersive Mushroom Kingdom theming.

text.en: Power-Up Bands sold separately at the entrance
Features Mario Kart: Bowser's Challenge
Toadstool Cafe serves themed meals

address.en: 100 Universal City Plaza, Universal City, CA 91608

hours.en: Varies by season — check Universal website

about.en: A themed retail store inside Super Nintendo World at Universal Studios Japan, designed to look like a factory from the Mushroom Kingdom with exclusive park merchandise.

address.en: Super Nintendo World, Universal Studios Japan, Osaka

about.en: A Mario Kart-themed shop near the Mario Kart ride exit in Super Nintendo World, selling racing-themed merchandise, apparel, and collectibles.

address.en: Super Nintendo World, Universal Studios Japan, Osaka

category.ja: レストラン / カフェ

about.en: A permanent Kirby-themed café in Tokyo Solamachi featuring adorable character-shaped dishes, themed drinks, and exclusive merchandise.

text.en: Reservations required — book online in advance
Every dish is shaped like a Kirby character
Exclusive café-only merchandise at the shop

address.en: Tokyo Solamachi 4F, 1-1-2 Oshiage, Sumida-ku, Tokyo

category.ja: レストラン / カフェ

about.en: An official Pokémon-themed café in Nihonbashi, Tokyo, serving character latte art, themed dishes, and offering exclusive tableware merchandise.

text.en: Advance reservation required via the Pokémon Café website
Chef Pikachu visits tables for photos
90-minute dining limit during peak hours

address.en: Nihonbashi Takashimaya S.C. East Bldg. 5F, 2-11-2 Nihonbashi, Chuo-ku, Tokyo

name.ja: ニンテンドーミュージアム

about.en: A museum in Uji, Kyoto dedicated to Nintendo's history, showcasing the company's evolution from playing cards to video games with interactive exhibits.

text.en: Timed-entry tickets required, book well in advance
Interactive exhibits let you play historic Nintendo products
Located in Uji, Kyoto — Nintendo's original headquarters area

address.en: 100 Kamikoma Torihane, Uji, Kyoto

about.en: A Nintendo-themed check-in lounge area at Narita International Airport where travelers can play games and take photos with Nintendo characters.

text.en: Free to visit, no ticket required
Play Nintendo Switch games while waiting for your flight

address.en: Narita International Airport Terminal 1, Narita, Chiba

category.ja: Nintendo Check Inステーション

about.en: A Nintendo-themed experience zone at Kansai International Airport featuring game stations, photo spots with Mario characters, and a small merchandise corner.

address.en: Kansai International Airport Terminal 1, Tajiri, Osaka

category.ja: 歴史的サイト & 企業

about.en: Nintendo's current corporate headquarters in Minami-ku, Kyoto. While not open to the public, the modern building is a pilgrimage site for fans worldwide.

text.en: Not open to the public — exterior viewing only
The building features subtle Nintendo design elements

address.en: 11-1 Kamitoba Hokodatecho, Minami-ku, Kyoto

category.ja: 歴史的サイト & 企業

about.en: Nintendo's original Meiji-era headquarters building in central Kyoto where Fusajiro Yamauchi first began producing hanafuda playing cards in 1889.

address.en: 342 Washio-cho, Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto
